# The boot path & kernel-crash forensics 🔴

**Goal.** Own every stage from firmware to a captured kernel panic: interrupt the boot loader, force and dissect a crash, then replace the firmware's boot logic with Linux itself.

**Audience:** you can bring up a Debian VM; comfort with GRUB/serial helps  
**Effort:** ⏱ half-day+  
**Prerequisites:** none

| # | Lab | You'll learn | Checkpoint (observable) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | [`vm-examples/vm-debian-amd64.toml`](../vm-examples/vm-debian-amd64.toml) | a baseline KVM cloud VM to experiment on. | `lab-vm.sh ssh` logs you in. |
| 2 | [`root-password-reset`](../root-password-reset/) 🔑 | console/boot access equals root — via `init=/bin/bash` or `rd.break`. | reset root on the VM; the OLD password is rejected and the NEW one works; `/proc/cmdline` proves your GRUB edit took (mind the serial char-drop gotcha). |
| 3 | [`kdump-kexec-lab`](../kdump-kexec-lab/) | arm kdump, force a panic, let the kexec capture kernel save a vmcore, then read it in `crash`. | `echo c > /proc/sysrq-trigger` yields a ~42 MB vmcore in `/var/crash`; `crash` `sym` resolves the panic to `test-module.c:8`. |
| 4 | [`linuxboot-uefi-kexec`](../linuxboot-uefi-kexec/) | firmware is just software: u-root as the firmware's `init`, which `kexec`s the real OS. | two 'Welcome to u-root!' banners + a `[0.000000]` clock reset (Tier C fast-loop → Tier B UKI on OVMF → Tier A coreboot ROM); the System-Transparency capstone boots ONLY a signed OSPKG and refuses a rogue one. |
| 5 | [`zfsbootmenu-boot-environments`](../zfsbootmenu-boot-environments/) | FreeBSD-style boot environments on Linux: ZFSBootMenu is a ZFS-capable Linux kernel packaged as an EFI executable that `kexec`s the OS straight off a ZFS clone, so you pick a whole-OS snapshot at boot — the `bectl` model, distro-agnostic (pool `bootfs` + `org.zfsbootmenu:commandline`). | host-safe suite green — `be.sh` emits the correct `zfs clone -o canmount=noauto -o mountpoint=/` + `zpool set bootfs=` plan with no pool; then under KVM, clone→`activate`→reboot boots the clone (`/proc/cmdline` shows `rpool/ROOT/testupgrade`) and re-activating the original recovers it.<br>🔎 auto-checkable → marker `4 passed` 🖥️ |

### Optional side-quests

- [`systemd261-nixos-measured-boot`](../systemd261-nixos-measured-boot/) — boot *integrity* rather than boot *recovery*: a Nix-built NixOS on **systemd 261** measured into a (soft)TPM, deployed on-disk over iPXE, with staged rollout by machine-ID (`ConditionFraction=`) and storage sealed to the measured state (TPM2 LUKS bound to PCR 7+11 + a PCR-quote attestation stub). Spikes A–G verified — measured-os MET on a dm-verity+UKI image, sealed LUKS unseals only on the expected PCRs; honest gap: `RestrictFileSystemAccess=` isn't in nixpkgs' systemd 261. See its PLAN.md.
